items.docs: The list of current BettingEvents for the given date. Events in this include market information but no outcomes will be included here. Intended to allow both visibility to Events in order to match up Events -> Games via the included GameID (where applicable) as well as provide a list of MarketIDs which are included in the given event.

params.description:
Year of the season (with optional season type).

items.docs: Returns the full list of BetttingEvents for the given season. Intended for those who need to tie BettingEventIDs to GameIDs.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets). Valid entries include: 134, 155, etc.

params.name: include
params.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: Returns the markets of all available types (e.g. Player Props, Team Props) and available outcomes for a given BettingEventID.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: marketId
params.description: The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull all outcomes/bets. Valid entries include: 421, 1041, etc.

items.docs: Returns full line movement for a given BettingMarket. Due to the sheer size of the output and the level of detail, it is intended for historical data purposes and not for the most up-to-the-second lines.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: season
params.description:
Year of the season
Examples: 2020, 2021, etc

items.docs: Returns available Futures outcomes for the given season. Does not include line movement.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets). Valid entries include: 134, 155, etc.

params.name: marketTypeID
params.description: The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for `Game Lines` 2 for `Player Props` 3 for `Team Props` 6 for `Game Props`

params.name: include
params.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: Returns Markets and available outcomes for a given event and market type requested. A lighter call than by BettingEventID as it only includes markets tagged with the specific MarketType, a full list of which is available for each sport in its Betting Metadata endpoint.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: gameID
params.description: The GameID of the desired game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets). Valid entries include: 14814

params.name: include
params.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: Returns the markets of all available types (e.g. Player Props, Team Props) and available outcomes for a given GameID.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: team
params.description:
The abbreviation of the requested team.
Examples: PHI, MIN, DET, etc.

items.docs: Describes recent team trends and performance against betting data in recent sets of games.

params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.description:
The abbreviation of the requested team.

params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

items.docs: Returns the list of MarketTypes, BetTypes, PeriodTypes, OutcomeTypes, and ResultTypes to map the IDs to descriptive names. Also includes a list of the MarketType, BetType & PeriodType combinations which we will have resulting for.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

items.docs: A list of all available sportsbooks with their associated unique IDs.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: marketId
params.description: The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull all outcomes/bets. Valid entries include: 406451, 404168, etc.

items.docs: Provide a market ID that supports resulting (i.e. has a ResultType) and this endpoint will return a result: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: marketId
params.description: The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull splits. MarketIds are pulled from the Betting Markets endpoints.

items.docs: List of Money and Bet Percentage splits for each outcome type available in this market. This specific endpoint will return the movement from this market as well as the most recent.

params.description: The ID of the game for which you want to receive splits for. GameIds are pulled from the Schedules and Games by Date endpoints.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: gameId
params.description: The unique GameID of the game in question.

params.name: include
params.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: This works in the same way as Betting Markets by Market Type but is prefiltered to the Player Props type only. Ideal if your application will only ever require Player Props, but if you also want Team Props etc. it is recommended to use the by Market Type endpoint.

items.docs: Returns the markets of all available types (e.g. Player Props, Team Props) and available outcomes for a given GameID. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets). Valid entries include: 134, 155, etc.

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

params.name: include
params.description:
This parameter indicates which BettingOutcome records to return in the payload. By default, this endpoint only returns available outcomes, excluding the unlisted bets.

Important Note: the default parameter of available is much faster and provides the best performance. Passing unlisted will return unlisted odds, but these are cached for much longer and may be several minutes old. For best performance, the default parameter is highly recommended.

Possible values include:

available - excludes any betting outcomes that have been removed/unlisted by the respective sportsbook. This is more lightweight and delivers odds much faster than using unlisted.

unlisted - includes the most recently available betting outcome per sportsbook, for each listed market, even those outcomes are expired/unlisted or otherwise unavailable from the respective sportsbook. This is a far heavier payload, and it's cached for much longer than available.

items.docs: Returns the markets and available outcomes for a given BettingEventID.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: marketId
params.description: The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull all outcomes/bets. Valid entries include: 421, 1041, etc.

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns full line movement for a given BettingMarket. Due to the sheer size of the output and the level of detail, it is intended for historical data purposes and not for the most up-to-the-second lines. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: marketId
params.description: The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull all outcomes/bets. Valid entries include: 406451, 404168, etc.

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Provide a market ID that supports resulting (i.e. has a ResultType) and this endpoint will return a result: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: season
params.description:
Year of the season
Examples: 2020, 2021, etc

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns available Futures markets for the given season. Does not include line movement. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

info.name: In-Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of an NBA game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 12780 or 12781

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns in-play game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for games on a given date. This also includes Resulting: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

info.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of an NBA game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 12780 or 12781

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns the game odds ( for games on a given date. In this endpoint both full-game and partial-game odds are included. This also includes Resulting: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parame

params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of an NBA game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14620, 16905, etc.

items.docs: Full statistical information for a specified game, down to the team and player stat level, delivered live during the game, called per individual game.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: date
params.description:
The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2015-JUL-31, 2015-SEP-01.

items.docs: Full statistical information for a specified game, down to the team and player stat level, delivered live during the games, called for all games on a given date.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: date
params.description:
The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2015-JUL-31, 2015-SEP-01.

params.name: minutes
params.description: Only returns player statistics that have changed in the last X minutes. You specify how many minutes in time to go back. Valid entries are: 1 or 2.

items.docs: This method returns all box scores for a given date, but only returns player stats that have changed in the last X minutes as specified in your API call. By definition this is a live endpoint, not final.

params.name: date
params.description:
The date of the game(s).
Examples: 2015-JUL-31, 2015-SEP-01.

Year of the season.
